Class Automaton

java.lang.Object
Automata.Models.Automaton
public class Automaton extends Object
Represents a finite automaton with states, final states and transitions.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Automaton(String id)
    Creates automaton with given id.

    Adds state to automaton.

    Adds transition between two states.

    Creates deterministic automaton from current automaton.

  • Constructor Details

    • Automaton

      public Automaton(String id)
      Creates automaton with given id.
      Parameters:
      id - automaton identifier

      public String getId()

    • getStates

      public ArrayList<String> getStates()

    • getFinalStates

      public ArrayList<String> getFinalStates()

    • getTransitions

      public ArrayList<Transition> getTransitions()

    • addState

      public void addState(String state)
      Adds state to automaton.
      Parameters:
      state - state name

    • addFinalState

      public void addFinalState(String state)
      Adds final state to automaton.
      Parameters:
      state - final state name

    • printInfo

      public void printInfo()
      Prints full information about the automaton.

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Checks if automaton language is empty.
      Returns:
      true if there are no final states

    • addTransition

      public void addTransition(String fromState, String symbol, String toState)
      Adds transition between two states.
      Parameters:
      fromState - start state
      symbol - transition symbol
      toState - destination state

    • isDeterministic

      public boolean isDeterministic()
      Checks if automaton is deterministic.
      Returns:
      true if automaton is deterministic

    • recognize

      public boolean recognize(String word)
      Checks if word is recognized by the automaton.
      Parameters:
      word - input word
      Returns:
      true if word is recognized

    • isFinite

      public boolean isFinite()
      Checks if automaton language is finite.
      Returns:
      true if language is finite

    • determinize

      public Automaton determinize(String newId)
      Creates deterministic automaton from current automaton.
      Parameters:
      newId - identifier of new automaton
      Returns:
      deterministic automaton
